attack_surface.data_description: Approval (CollateralTokenRouter, infinity) or burn(Alice, amount) calls

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of rate limiting or transaction volume controls on user-initiated token operations (approve, burn) could allow a malicious actor to exhaust network resources or contract gas limits.

actor_risk.description: A malicious user could intentionally or unintentionally trigger excessive transactions (e.g., approvals, burns), impacting network and contract availability for others.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Excessive transactions can lead to increased gas fees, network congestion, and temporary unavailability of contract functions for other legitimate users.

assumptions: Smart contracts (TokenRouter, ERC20, ERC4626 Vault) do not implement sufficient rate-limiting or transaction volume controls.
Network gas fees are not prohibitive enough to prevent such attacks from being economically viable.

mitigation.strategy: Implement on-chain rate limiting or transaction volume caps for user-initiated token operations (e.g., per-address limits, cooldowns).

description: A malicious user could flood the network with excessive token approval or burn transactions via the TokenRouter, leading to resource exhaustion, increased gas costs, and denial of service for other legitimate users interacting with the protocol.

trace_source.doc_reference: solidity/contracts/token/Hyperlane Tokens and Warp Routes#Remote Transfer Lifecycle Diagrams
